title: oxytropis nigrescens subsp. bryophila
intro: Oxytropis nigrescens subsp. bryophila, also known as the alpine globe milkvetch, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the pea family Fabaceae. It is native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, improving digestion, and supporting immune function. Oxytropis nigrescens subsp. bryophila can be prepared as a herbal tea or tincture, and it has also been used in combination with other herbs to create medicinal remedies.
